title: echinospartum ibericum subsp. pulviniformis
intro: Echinospartum ibericum subsp. pulviniformis, also known as Spanish buckthorn, is a shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its thorny branches and small, white flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its anthraquinone content, which acts as a laxative. The health benefits of Spanish buckthorn include its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried extract, capsule, or tea, with recommended dosages varying depending on the specific health concern being addressed.
